steps.title: Identify the shift from general claim to specific examples
steps.content: The passage moves from the broad statement "Comics by other Black cartoonists followed" directly to naming specific cartoonists (Robb Armstrong and Keith Knight) with their years. The transition must bridge this shift.

steps.title: Apply the general-to-specific pattern
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example — a general claim about Black cartoonists is followed by specific named cartoonists. The blank needs a transition signaling a concrete instance.
  • Choice A — Previously,: signals something happened earlier in time, so fails — the named cartoonists follow chronologically, not precede the general claim. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: signals contrast, so fails — the named cartoonists support the general claim, not contradict it. ✗
  • Choice C — Alternatively,: signals a different option or substitute, so fails — the examples are not alternatives to anything. ✗
  • Choice D — For instance,: signals a specific example of the preceding general claim, so fits. ✓
That is the correct answer.
